task_completion.description: Verify that the YDBGui application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the ^XMB(3.8) global in YDBGui.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The string '^XMB(3.8)' is visible in the global search bar, input field, or selected in the global directory list within the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the contents of the ^XMB(3.8) global are displayed in the viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main data panel or tree view of YDBGui displays the nodes and values for ^XMB(3.8), showing actual mail group data.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation or opening of the output text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) is open with 'mail_groups_audit.txt' visible in the title bar, tab, or file path.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 10 mail group names are recorded in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor displays a list containing at least 10 distinct mail group names.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the YDBGui application to find the information, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the YDBGui graphical interface to view the global, without terminal commands like 'mumps', 'zwrite', or direct database queries being used to extract the list.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not write a script to bypass the GUI requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of Python, Bash, or M (MUMPS) scripts being written or executed in the terminal to dump the contents of ^XMB(3.8).

integrity.description: Verify that the recorded mail groups appear to be genuinely extracted from the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain actual VistA mail group names (often uppercase, system-related names) rather than generic placeholders (e.g., 'Group 1', 'Test Group') or a direct copy-paste of the task prompt.
